cases.description: Refactor request triggers workflow; baseline phase is mandatory
cases.prompt: Refactor the order intake module to be deeper.
expectations: Skill refactor-workflow is selected
Workflow starts at phase intent
Phase baseline is MANDATORY before plan — captures current behavior with characterization tests
Agent does NOT skip baseline even if existing tests look comprehensive
Agent invokes devloop:worktrees for an isolated worktree

cases.description: Refactor MUST NOT change observable behavior
cases.prompt: While refactoring, I noticed a bug. Fix it as part of the refactor.
expectations: Agent refuses to fix bugs during refactor
Agent explains: refactor success criterion is 'no behavior change'
Agent files the bug as a follow-up bug-fix-workflow
Agent's commit message says 'Refactor: no behavior change'

cases.description: Phase baseline writes characterization tests for uncovered behaviors
cases.prompt: Set up the baseline for this refactor.
expectations: Agent inventories existing tests and confirms they assert behavior (not implementation)
Agent identifies gaps where observable behaviors are not covered
Agent writes characterization tests for each uncovered behavior
Tests assert what the code currently does (whether or not 'correct')
All baseline tests pass before phase plan begins

cases.description: Phase plan applies deletion test on each proposed module
expectations: Agent applies the deletion test on every module in scope
If deleting concentrates complexity → module earns its keep (deep)
If deleting just moves complexity → module is shallow, should be merged
Plan includes Module depth analysis section with deepening rationale
Plan includes Seams introduced or removed section

cases.description: Phase execute lands the refactor in small commits; tests stay green throughout
expectations: Agent moves code in stages: add new shape → update callers → remove old shape
Each commit leaves the codebase compiling and all baseline tests passing
If a baseline test fails, agent STOPS and investigates
If multi-step (≥3 distinct steps), agent uses subagent-orchestration mode sequential
